# Political Campaign Office Operations — Nigeria-First Research Brief

**Niche ID:** P3-campaign-office-campaign-office-ops
**Vertical slug:** campaign-office
**VN-ID:** VN-POL-CAM-001
**Niche family:** NF-POL-CAM standalone
**Family role:** standalone
**Research date:** 2026-04-26

## 1. Nigerian Business Reality
Nigerian political campaigns (INEC election cycles: Presidential, Governorship, NASS, State Assembly, LGA) are high-intensity operations involving ward coordinators, polling unit agents (POAs), logistics teams, media liaisons, and voter contact programmes. The Electoral Act 2022 introduced stricter campaign finance disclosure requirements. The 2023 election cycle saw unprecedented use of digital tools (WhatsApp broadcasts, Telegram, Twitter/X) for grassroots mobilisation. Campaign websites serve as the official public record for candidates — manifesto, press releases, volunteer registration, and donation transparency.

## 2. Target Audience Profile
- **Ward Coordinator, 45, Ogun State** — Needs to check rally schedule, download campaign materials, and reach campaign director on WhatsApp.
- **Journalist, 32, Channels TV** — Looking for candidate's official manifesto, biography, and press office contact.
- **Volunteer, 24, NYSC member, Enugu** — Wants to register as a volunteer, join the WhatsApp broadcast list, and find upcoming events.

## 3. Recommended Website Structure
- **Home:** Candidate hero image, slogan, key policy pillars, donate/volunteer CTA
- **Candidate:** Biography, political track record, endorsements
- **Manifesto:** Policy positions (economy, security, education, health) — section by section
- **Newsroom:** Press releases, speech transcripts, media coverage
- **Volunteer:** Registration form, ward/LGA coordinator network, WhatsApp group link
- **Campaign Finance:** Donor transparency statement (INEC Electoral Act 2022 §§95–105)
- **Contact:** Press office WhatsApp, phone, address, NDPR notice

## 4. Content Tone & Voice
Compelling, patriotic, action-oriented. "Together we can transform {state/constituency}" register. Reference INEC, ward/LGA structure, NYSC volunteers. Primary CTA: "Join the Movement". Secondary CTA: "Read the Manifesto". WhatsApp: "Contact Campaign Headquarters".

## 5. Trust Signal Inventory
- INEC candidacy certification confirmation
- Political party (APC, PDP, LP, NNPP) official nomination letter
- Electoral Act 2022 campaign finance compliance statement
- CAC/INEC registered NGO for support groups
- Media endorsements (newspaper op-eds, television appearances)
- NDPR compliance for volunteer and donor data

## 6. Image Direction (5-7 Specific Concepts)
1. Candidate at podium addressing rally — large crowd, Nigerian flags, state signage
2. Ward coordinator meeting — grassroots organisers in community hall
3. Campaign vehicle convoy — branded vehicles, volunteers cheering
4. Candidate visiting constituents — market, school, or health centre
5. Manifesto launch event — candidates with party officials, formal stage
6. Volunteer registration booth — NYSC members signing up
7. Campaign office interior — coordination room with maps, volunteers working

## 7. Regulatory Compliance Checklist
- INEC Electoral Act 2022 compliance notice displayed
- Campaign finance disclosure statement (§§95–105)
- NDPR consent for volunteer/donor data (mandatory)
- No incitement or defamatory content compliance policy
- Political party affiliation clearly stated (avoid INEC code violations)

## 8. Nigeria-First Compliance Checklist
- [x] WhatsApp CTA for campaign headquarters and volunteer coordination
- [x] All donation/event amounts in NGN (₦) only
- [x] Nigerian electoral system context (INEC, ward/LGA, NYSC)
- [x] Nigerian names in volunteer testimonials
- [x] INEC compliance trust signals displayed
- [x] Payment: Paystack, bank transfer for campaign donations
- [x] Mobile-first 375px layout
- [x] esc() on all user-supplied strings
- [x] NDPR notice on volunteer registration and donation forms
